one_liner: Mailchimp's Marketing API (v3, REST) offers broad, self-serve API key access on all plans plus OAuth2 for third-party integrations, with several community-built MCP servers already available.

auth_methods.notes: Self-serve API key generated from account settings; used via HTTP Basic Auth (key as password) or Bearer auth.
Authorization Code flow for accessing data on behalf of other Mailchimp users; requires registering an app and redirect URI.

self_serve_notes: API key can be generated immediately from account settings on any plan tier, including Free. OAuth2 requires app registration but is also self-serve (no partnership approval needed). Some advanced features (e.g., Transactional API) require paid plans.

api_surface.notes: Marketing API v3 covers audiences/contacts, campaigns, reports, e-commerce, webhooks, batch/async requests; separate Transactional API and Export API also exist; official SDKs for PHP, Python, Node, etc.

evidence.supports: Describes API key generation, REST structure, API versions, and self-serve access tied to account plan.
Details OAuth2 Authorization Code flow for accessing data on behalf of other users vs. using API key directly.
Quick start guide for generating API key and making first API calls.
Documents REST API methods and parameters, indicating broad API surface.
Official Python SDK for Marketing API, indicating mature API surface and existing tooling ecosystem referenced by MCP servers.

flags: Multiple third-party/community MCP servers exist but no single official Mailchimp-maintained MCP server confirmed
Some advanced features (Transactional API) gated behind higher-tier plans
